subject: Phase 1 Progress — Warren Buffett Intelligence Gathering
message:
Phase 1 is running. Here's what I've gathered so far and what I'm still working on.

**CONFIRMED — Done:**
- Complete career timeline (birth 1930 → CEO succession announcement May 2025, Greg Abel named successor)
- Full position history: Buffett-Falk → Graham-Newman → Buffett Partnership → Berkshire Hathaway control 1965 → chairman 1970
- Investment milestones: GEICO 1951, See's Candy 1972, Washington Post 1973, Coca-Cola 1988, BNSF 2009, Apple 2016+
- Investment philosophy framework (Graham-originated → Munger-evolved): intrinsic value, margin of safety, Mr. Market, economic moat, owner earnings
- Key relationships: Charlie Munger (1959–2023), Bill Gates (friend, Giving Pledge co-founder), Benjamin Graham (mentor), Katharine Graham, Ajit Jain
- Philanthropy: 99% pledge, Gates Foundation, The Giving Pledge (2010), Susan Thompson Buffett Foundation
- Voice evidence: Berkshire Hathaway shareholder letters 1977–2024 (official source), Warren Buffett Archive (warrenbuffett.com — 145 hours video + transcripts), "The Essays of Warren Buffett" (Lawrence Cunningham, 4 editions)

**CONFIRMED — Key Buffett Voice Signals:**
- "Charlie Munger is the architect; I was merely the contractor." (2024 letter)
- "This system is called capitalism. It has its faults and abuses...but it also can work wonders unmatched by other economic systems." (2024 letter)
- "The luckiest day of my life was being born American." (Berkshire meetings)
- Known for: self-deprecating humor, storytelling in letters, direct plain-English prose, frugality as personal identity

**STILL GATHERING:**
- Specific video/audio transcriptions from warrenbuffett.com (PDF letters not readable; HTML archive not accessible — finding alternate text sources)
- Attribution verification for widely-circulated quotes ("Be fearful when others are greedy" — source disputed)
- Berkshire Hathaway specific position data (current top holdings, 13F filings)
- More on See's Candy, Geico, and specific "moat" case studies with dates
- Teaching: University of Nebraska night class (age ~20, students were older than him), fireside chat style

**PLANNED:**
- Once I have ~10 research themes mapped, I'll spawn avatar specialists for each domain
- Domains likely: (1) value investing philosophy, (2) Berkshire Hathaway operations, (3) Buffett's voice and letter-writing craft, (4) Berkshire insurance subsidiaries, (5) Coca-Cola/AAPL investment cases, (6) philanthropy and governance, (7) Buffett-Munger relationship

I'll keep working and report again when I hit the next milestone. Any specific focus areas you'd like prioritized first?
